name: Sweet corn
why_now: Corn sugars convert to starch quickly after picking. July corn eaten within a day or two of harvest is a different ingredient from corn that has traveled for a week. Grocery stores turn over fast in summer — the corn on the shelf right now is likely good.
how_to_choose: Husk tightly wrapped and bright green
Silk slightly dry at the top, not slimy
Plump kernels felt through the husk
Avoid any ears with dried-out or blackened husks
price_note: One of the least expensive vegetables of summer. Widely available at any grocery store in July and often on sale.
buy_this_much: 2–3 ears for one person mid-week, another 1–2 for the weekend

flavor_paths.name: Bright Taco
flavor_paths.description: Lime, taco seasoning, tomato, beans, cilantro.

flavor_paths.name: Italian Summer
flavor_paths.description: Basil, olive oil, garlic. Good off the cob into a bowl.

flavor_paths.name: Chili-crisp bowl
flavor_paths.description: Sesame, chili crisp, and a little soy over rice.

weekday_uses: Cut off the cob into a rice bowl
Added to taco bowls with beans and tomato
Quick corn and tomato salad with basil
weekend_use: Corn salad alongside grilled chicken thighs and peaches.
storage: Keep husked corn refrigerated and use within two days. Husked is fine for a day, unhusked is better if waiting.
one_thing_to_learn: Fresh corn is good raw. Shaved directly off the cob into a salad, it has a sweetness that disappears quickly with heat.
